name: Saudi Arabia
summary: Day 63 Thursday. SAUDI CONSPICUOUS-QUIET ON UAE OPEC EXIT NOW 3 DAYS RUNNING (TUE-WED-THU) = CROSSES THRESHOLD FROM 'CONSIDERING RESPONSE' TO 'NO GOOD RESPONSE'; JEDDAH SUMMIT UAE SENT FM NOT PRESIDENT (DIPLOMATIC SNUB); MBS NO DIRECT STATEMENT; AL-SABBAN AL JAZEERA DEFLECTIVE. Saudi formal-response watching trigger from Apr 28 brief at Day 3 of 14: NO MBS direct statement, operational evidence of rift in summit attendance, Saudi proxy commentary deflecting rather than countering. Saudi former oil adviser Mohammad al-Sabban via Al Jazeera ('not a major blow, one country going out doesn't mean anything') — but US News analysis 'UAE Oil Break Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift as Gulf Power Shifts' frames Riyadh as having 'no good public options.' Soufan Center IntelBrief calls it 'Gulf Shakeup.' Atlantic Council 'Petrodollar Crisis' framing. UAE OPEC exit takes effect TOMORROW MAY 1 — calendar inflection ~24 hours away. With UAE OUT, the June ministerial decision on production discipline now rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait coordination ALONE. Brent closes $118-120 (+6-8%, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED) priced ~20% UAE-OPEC fragmentation contributor. SADARA JV shutdown unchanged: indefinitely shut, all 26 manufacturing units idle. June 15 grace period expiry trigger UNCHANGED — Aramco backstops $2.4B / Dow $1.3B of $3.7B principal. Aramco Q1 disclosure May 6 is the next major Saudi-corporate read (will set Sadara provision + Hormuz-impact tone + potentially Saudi-on-UAE-OPEC framing). Aramco May OSP +$19.50/bbl premium pricing-restraint signal continues. Saudi production -23% MoM (10.1 → 7.8 MBPD) structural Hormuz-export suppression continues. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
